name: Venison Jerky
recipeIngredient: A few grains of cayenne pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. Freeze venison until firm and solid enough to slice easily.
2. Cut 1/8-inch slices with a sharp knife or slicer, then cut slices into strips that are 1 & 1/2 inches wide.
3. In a bowl, blend the remaining ingredients together.
4. Place the marinated venison strips in rows on a shallow baking pan.
5. Refrigerate and let marinate overnight.
6. Drain well.
7. Dehydrator: Cover trays with strips without overlapping. Dry for 4 hours at 140°F. Turn and rotate the trays, then dry another 6 to 8 hours. Well-dried jerky should be dark and fibrous looking and brittle enough to splinter when bent in two.
8. Sun drying is not recommended in most climates.
9. Oven: Lay strips of marinated meat in rows over trays, being careful not to overlap the strips. Dry at 110°F until the edges will split when bent in two, which takes 18 to 24 hours.
